sources.title: THE ANNA AKHMATOVA FILE 1989
sources.description: Title: The Anna Achmatova File - Lichnoe delo Anny Akhmatovoy (1990) Directed by: Semyon Aranovich Date of birth: 23 July 1934, Derazhnya, Khmelnytskyy province, Soviet Union Date of death: 1995 (age 60) Writing credits: Semyon Aranovich, Yelena Ignatova Music: Sofiya Gubajdulina, Aleksandr Knaifel Year: 1990 Country: Soviet Union Language: Russian Color: Color | Black and White Runtime: 65 min. Synopsis An impressionistic look at the life of Russian poet, Anna Akhmatova, 1889-1966. It begins and ends with footage from her funeral, and includes readings from her diaries and of her poems. Also included are passages of official Soviet criticism.

metadata.Plot: An impressionistic look at the life of Russian poet, Anna Akhmatova, 1889-1966. It begins and ends with footage from her funeral, and includes readings from her diaries and of her poems. Also included are passages of official Soviet criticism. She was born near Odessa, married and published her first volume of poetry in 1912, was a friend of Blok, Mandelshtam, Pasternak, and Solzhenitsyn, and recounts an extraordinary day with Marina Tsvetaeva about a month before Tsvetaeva's death. Her husband Nikolai Gumilyov was shot in 1921, the same month Blok died. Her only son, Lev, was imprisoned in the Stalin era. Recognition came after many sorrows, too late to be sweet.
metadata.Actors: Anna Akhmatova, Leonid Brezhnev, Joseph Brodsky
